pages.raw_text:
Attorney Fred n. Moore stated
over the telephone from Occur
d'Alene today that a motion for
rev trial in the FiIigno case would
be filed tomorrow. He said that
he did not desire Miss Elizabeth
GurIey Flynn to make any state
ment at this time regarding the
outcome Of the trial.


Acquittal for Elizabeth Gurley
Flynn Jones and conviction for c.
l.. Filigno was the verdict render
ed at 10 o'clock last night by the
jury in the l. w. w. conspiracy"
cases. 1t took exactly "a hours
for the jury to arrive at a conclu-
sion, and then only compromise
was effected


From Wednesday night, when
the case was given to the jury, I'M
til 5 o'clock yesterday afternoon,
the jury stood seven for conviction
and five for the acquittal of both
defendants. Following the report
